characters.name: Amy Sanders
characters.description: MirandaGordo Amanda "Amy" Sandersis Kate's cousin. She is in a band and is not very responsible. She is practically an older version of Kate. Kate and Amy seem to have a rocky relationship. In the episodeParty Over Here, she didn't even know that it was Kate's birthday. When Amy brings out a cake, Kate is disappointed to find that it was a strawberry cake instead of a chocolate cake, there was no candles and no one sings "Happy Birthday." Kate is shown to have a rocky relationship with Amy.

characters.name: Angel Lieberman
characters.description: Angel Leibermanis the stereotype of the Goth/bad girl. She ditches school, chews gum in class, and frequently gets detention. She is first seen in Mrs. Wortman's Algebra class. Angel tries to copy off of Lizzie's pop quiz when the teacher isn't looking and ends up getting caught and sent to detention. At first, she is furious at Lizzie, but feels better when Lizzie says that she will let Angel cheat. Angel comes up with a lot of acronyms like LL (live large) and EE (the two letters in "geek" and "dweeb"). She calls Lizzie "Frizz" and Gordo "Gordork." She is a fan of the rap singer Eminem and wants to start her own band.[1]

characters.name: Animated Lizzie
characters.description: Animated Lizzieis an animated alter ego ofLizzie McGuire. She is a representation of the live Lizzie's thoughts. Her animation is a caricature of Hilary Duff, the actress who portrays Lizzie. Animated Lizzie is a small and slim 13-year-old teenage girl with light fair skin and straight, shoulder-length blonde hair in a long bob cut. Animated Lizzie wears a bright hot pink cropped tank top with blue capri pants and orange chunky platform flip-flops to make her look taller.

characters.name: Brooke Baker
characters.description: Brooke Bakeris a girl inLizzie's school. In "Gordo and the Girl", she wasGordo's girlfriend until he broke up with her at the end of the episode. She was played by Kyla Pratt.

characters.name: Claire Miller
characters.description: Claire Milleris the secondary antagonist inLizzie McGuire. Claire is the Head Cheerleader and yearbook editor[1]atHillridge Junior High School. She becameKate Sanders'friend after Kate stopped being friends withLizzie. Even though Kate is usually the more dominant of the two, Claire has shown to be more vindictive than Kate, and often pressures her not to renew her friendship with Lizzie, perhaps out of jealousy. Claire is the Head Cheerleader and yearbook editor[1]atHillridge Junior High School. She becameKate Sanders'friend after Kate stopped being friends withLizzie. Even though Kate is usually the more dominant of the two, Claire has shown to be more vindictive than Kate, and often pressures her not to renew her friendship with Lizzie, perhaps out of jealousy. At one point,Gordodevelops a crush on Claire, and she is also friends with Gordo's one-time girlfriend,Brooke. She is first seen inRumors, when she judges Lizzie for the cheerleading squad. InBetween a Rock and a Bra Place, Claire shops withher mom, who's shown to have a much nicer personality than her daughter and is seemingly unaware of her daughter's cold attitude towards others. Claire is also in Lizzie's science class. Claire, Kate, and Larry participated in Fact-athalon, and won. Claire is known as "Clean Tween" as said inDear Lizzie. Claire is left-handed.[2]

characters.name: Coach Kelly
characters.description: Coach Kellyis the gym teacher atHillridge Junior High School. Miranda nicknames her "The Evil Sorceress of P.E." and Lizzie calls her "Queen of the Gym-nauseum." She's played byDot Marie-Jones.

characters.name: Danny Kessler
characters.description: Daniel "Danny" Kessleris a student at Hillridge Junior High School. He is the teen heartthrob of 7th grade, followed byEthan Craft. Season 1

characters.name: Danny Kessler

characters.name: David Gordon
characters.description: David Zephyr[2]"Gordo" Gordonis the best friend of bothLizzieandMiranda. He usually offers sarcasm and good advice. He is very intelligent, practical, and he is a "straight A" student. He is of the Jewish faith.[3]He has a crush on Lizzie,[4]. His birthday is July 28. Gordo's parents are both psychiatrists.[5] Gordo is the archetype of the nonconformist, as he prefers to be different from everybody else and follow his own path.[2] He likes to film videos and wants to be a movie director, like Steven Spielberg. Gordo is always loyal to his friends, and his quick thinking often gets them out of trouble. He does not understand all the "girly" stuff that Miranda and Lizzie do. Gordo is sarcastic, caring and intelligent; he can also be goofy and witty. Gordo is a fan of Tyra Banks.[9] Gordo likes to watch foreign films.[2] Gordo likes rice pudding.[10] Gordo doesn't like dancing.[2] Gordo likes "The Mummy" movie.[7]Whether this is the original Universal movie, its 1999 remake, or the Hammer Studios version is unclear.

characters.name: Eduardo Sanchez
characters.description: Eduardo SanchezisMiranda'sfather. He is married toDaniela Sanchez.

characters.name: Ethan Craft
characters.description: Ethan Craftwas the boy whoLizzie,Miranda, and most of the other girls at their school have a huge crush on. He didn't appear to be very bright, but he was kind and friendly to everyone (although in the first season, he was portrayed as more of a bad boy/class bully, and once forced Gordo to do things against his will). In themovie, he andKateget together. Ethan is an accomplished mini-golfer.[1] When Ethan was younger, his father lost his job for an extended period of time, and Ethan learned what it was like to go without.[1] Lizzie McGuire & Miranda Sanchez Ethan likes the girls, but only as friends. Gordo Ethan used to be really mean to Gordo early in the series, but they eventually become friends. Despite that, Gordo's jealous of Ethan because he (Gordo) had a crush on Lizzie, but she (Lizzie) had a crush on Ethan. Kate Sanders They get together in the movie, but it's implied by Kate at the beginning that they had actually dated before but broke up (also implying that they may have anon/off relationship). Ethan has at least two older brothers.[2]
characters.personality_section: Lizzie McGuire & Miranda Sanchez Ethan likes the girls, but only as friends. characters.name: Gammy McGuire
characters.description: Gammy McGuireisSam'smother, making herLizzieandMatt'spaternal grandmother. She is 61 years old.[1] She teaches windsurfing.[1] She is renowned for her spare ribs recipe.[2] She teaches windsurfing.[1] She is renowned for her spare ribs recipe.[2]
characters.personality_section: She teaches windsurfing.[1] She is renowned for her spare ribs recipe.[2]

characters.name: Isabella Parigi
characters.description: Isabella Parigiis an Italian pop star and a secondary character in theLizzie McGuire The Movie(also played by Hillary Duff). Isabella and Lizzie look identical to each other, with the only real physical difference being their hair colors (Isabella's a brunette while Lizzie's a blonde). Isabella and her singing partner/boyfriend,Paolo Valisari, were supposed to present an award at the International Music Video Awards, but they had a falling out, possibly due to Paolo's lip synching. To avoid being sued for a contract breach, Paolo took matters into his own hands. Paolo meets Lizzie when she and her class were on a field trip to Rome, and takes notice of her resemblance to Isabella, and decides to use it to make it seem that Isabella's lip synching, telling her that it is required. The media takes notice, and Paolo simply claims Lizzie is Isabella with dyed blonde hair. Isabella and her singing partner/boyfriend,Paolo Valisari, were supposed to present an award at the International Music Video Awards, but they had a falling out, possibly due to Paolo's lip synching. To avoid being sued for a contract breach, Paolo took matters into his own hands. Paolo meets Lizzie when she and her class were on a field trip to Rome, and takes notice of her resemblance to Isabella, and decides to use it to make it seem that Isabella's lip synching, telling her that it is required. The media takes notice, and Paolo simply claims Lizzie is Isabella with dyed blonde hair. SinceGordowas covering for Lizzie and tells their principal, Miss Ungermeyer, that he has been sneaking out on the trips, Gordo heads to the airport, he meets the real Isabella (who was upset to learn she was being impersonated) and discovers the truth about Paolo. Gordo explains the situation to her and they head to the IMVA being held at the Colosseum, where they tell Lizzie the truth. At first, Lizzie refuses to believe it, but Isabella encourages Lizzie to trust Gordo over Paolo, especially since Lizzie and Gordo have known each other for basically their entire lives while Lizzie's only known Paolo for less than a month. Isabella and Gordo pull the plug on the record tape, exposing Paolo as a fraud. Isabella further humiliates him by going on stage when Lizzie was already on. As Paolo leaves, Isabella introduces Lizzie to the crowd and has her perform her and Paolo's songWhat Dreams Are Made Ofin her place.

characters.name: Jo McGuire
characters.description: Josephine Lynn "Jo" McGuireisLizzieandMatt'smother. Jo was born in Walla Walla, Washington, and is of Swiss, German, Hungarian and Polish descent.[3] As a child, Jo was a geeky girl who envied anybody that was popular. Jo cares deeply about Lizzie but is trying to figure out how to raise a teenager. As a result, she sometimes tends to "mess up" situations in Lizzie's life while trying to make them better. Jo is a big fan of Chinese food.[10] Jo hates softball.[11] Jo occasionally reads to people at a convalescent home.[3]

characters.name: Lanny Onasis
characters.description: Lanny OnasisisMatt'sbest friend. He never talks, but Matt has no problem communicating with him.SamandJobelieve that he is shy, but it is more likely that he simply chooses not to talk.[1]  He is a direct descendant of Crispus Attucks. Lanny attends Hillridge Elementary.[2] Pumpkin is the only flavor of ice cream that he eats.[1] Lanny is a member of his church choir.[3] Eating spare ribs gives Lanny nightmares about walruses.[3] Lanny operates a website that generates revenue.[4]

characters.name: Larry Tudgeman
characters.description: Larry Tudgeman Lawrence "Larry" Tudgeman IIIis the stereotype of the nerdy student atHillridge Junior High School. He is the Class President, having defeated Lizzie and Head CheerleaderClaire Miller.[1]He was played byKyle Downes. Although he is ridiculed and taunted by fellow students, Larry has a far superior image of himself and sees himself as a sophisticated alpha-male instead of eccentric, obsessive and lacking in hygiene (which most people see him as because he has worn the same shirt since the fourth grade). Although he is ridiculed and taunted by fellow students, Larry has a far superior image of himself and sees himself as a sophisticated alpha-male instead of eccentric, obsessive and lacking in hygiene (which most people see him as because he has worn the same shirt since the fourth grade). He is also obsessed with science fiction movies and television programs. This is conveyed in the Season 1 episode “The Untitled Stan Jansen Project” when Gordo is setting him up and asks him to describe himself. He replies with, “I’ve always seen myself as a Captain Kirk meets James Bond type of guy... going where no secret agent has ever been before.” When Gordo reveals to him that he is a “geek,” Larry gets extremely offended and exclaims, “No, I’m not! My mom says the other kids are mean to me because they’rejealous!” This is also conveyed in “Dear Lizzie,” where it is shown that Larry sees himself in a much more superior character, and signed his email to Lizzie as The Captain With No Shipmates. However, Larry states many times that he is proud of his individuality. In “Scarlet Larry,” he explains that his intelligence comes from his interest of the world in general. Like Gordo, Larry, despite appearing creepy and erratic, is more comfortable in his own skin when compared to Lizzie, Miranda, Kate, and his other fellow students, who constantly worry about their looks and what others think of them, and isn’t so bothered about how others perceive him. However, this does not mean to say that he does not want to be friends with people who are considered “popular.” Throughout the series, he tried hard to be friends with Kate — not because of wanting a taste of popularity, but more because of the fact that he might have had a crush on her. This can be conveyed in “I’ve Got Rhythmic,” where Kate persuades Larry into ruining Lizzie’s performance by flirting with him. However, the chances of a further relationship are ruined in “I Do, I Don’t” when Kate and Larry have to work together as in a fake “marriage” and Kate is much more interested in having Ethan as a partner; instead of working with Larry, she tries to persuade Ethan to “leave” Miranda, his “wife,” for her. When Kate “leaves” Larry at the marriage reunion in school, she humiliates him in front of everyone. Then, after a heated argument, Larry gets his revenge on her by pouring a bowl of punch on her head, soaking her. Following this, Larry appears to develop a mild crush on Miranda. He has a pi
characters.personality_section: Although he is ridiculed and taunted by fellow students, Larry has a far superior image of himself and sees himself as a sophisticated alpha-male instead of eccentric, obsessive and lacking in hygiene (which most people see him as because he has worn the same shirt since the fourth grade). He is also obsessed with science fiction movies and television programs. This is conveyed in the Season 1 episode “The Untitled Stan Jansen Project” when Gordo is setting him up and asks him to describe himself. He replies with, “I’ve always seen myself as a Captain Kirk meets James Bond type of guy... going where no secret agent has ever been before.” When Gordo reveals to him that he is a “geek,” Larry gets extremely offended and exclaims, “No, I’m not! My mom says the other kids are mean to me because they’rejealous!” This is also conveyed in “Dear Lizzie,” where it is shown that Larry sees himself in a much more superior character, and signed his email to Lizzie as The Captain With No Shipmates. However, Larry states many times that he is proud of his individuality. In “Scarlet Larry,” he explains that his intelligence comes from his interest of the world in general. Like Gordo, Larry, despite appearing creepy and erratic, is more comfortable in his own skin when compared to Lizzie, Miranda, Kate, and his other fellow students, who constantly worry about their looks and what others think of them, and isn’t so bothered about how others perceive him. characters.name: Larry Tudgeman

Although he is ridiculed and taunted by fellow students, Larry has a far superior image of himself and sees himself as a sophisticated alpha-male instead of eccentric, obsessive and lacking in hygiene (which most people see him as because he has worn the same shirt since the fourth grade). characters.name: Lizzie McGuire (Character)
characters.description: This article is for the Lizzie McGuire character. Elizabeth Brooke "Lizzie" McGuireis the main protagonist in the show & movie. She is a caring, rational and cooperative person, although her animated alter-ego displays her true feelings from time to time. As the show progresses, she undergoes various "image" changes, including becoming rebellious, disrespectful and rude.[1]Lizzie constantly gets into arguments with her younger brother, Matt, but is well-aware of what is right and wrong. Lizzie has a cousin named Heather.[2] Lizzie is a sweet, honest teenage girl, who always knows when to do the right thing. She sticks up for her friends, but sometimes she needs reminding to as shown in some episodes. She is very kind and is even nice to her enemies, such asKate Sanders. All Lizzie wants to do is get by life one step at a time. She is always trying to impress her crushes, such asEthan Craft. Sometimes when she does something that gets her temporarily famous she decides that this sort of life is not for her and gives it up, as shown during the time she becomes a teen model and the time she dated a celebrity. Lizzie is allergic to oyster sauce. She once said that she hates Matt so much that she believed that she is allergic tohim. She has two best friends,GordoandMiranda. She lives just a few minutes walk from Gordo.[4]Gordoappears to have a crush on her,[11]but she remains unaware of that until they graduate from junior high.[12] At one time, Kate was Lizzie's best friend, until she became popular. They renewed the friendship during a trip to Rome.[13] Facts about Lizzie: Lizzie is allergic to strawberry ice cream.[14]Lizzie has a stuffed pig named Mr. Snuggles.[3]Lizzie has clear ideas for her future. She wants to live in a nice place, travel a lot, and have time for her twin children.[5]Lizzie's aptitude test suggests a career as a cosmetologist.[5]Lizzie enjoys making candles.[15]Lizzie is the only member of the McGuire family who can work Picture-in-Picture on the television.[15]Lizzie usually sleeps until noon on the weekends.[15]Lizzie likes peanut butter and jelly sandwiches, with the crusts cut off in a heart shape.[16][17]Lizzie is on the staff of the school's web-zine.[18] As a thirteen-year-old, Lizzie looks forward to going on a real date, learning to drive, and dying her hair.[15] Lizzie's favorite afternoon pick-me-up is a smoothie,[19]and oven-fried chicken is one of Lizzie's favorite foods.[19] Lizzie doesn't like the beach[20]and hates monster movies.[20] Classes that Lizzie takes in the seventh grade: Classes that Lizzie takes in the eighth grade:

characters.name: Matt McGuire
characters.description: Matthew "Matt" McGuireis Lizzie's younger brother. Matt has a cousin named Heather.[3] Matt is a student at Hillridge Elementary.[5]He is a scheming, witty, cheeky young prankster who goes to extreme lengths to continuously annoy and irritate Lizzie. At school, he is the class clown. He can be lazy, and, although he does have many potential talents, he doesn't use them much. His best friend isLanny Onasis, who never speaks, but Matt can understand him anyway. Matt is sometimes menaced by a bully named Heywood Biggs. Matt is a student at Hillridge Elementary.[5]He is a scheming, witty, cheeky young prankster who goes to extreme lengths to continuously annoy and irritate Lizzie. At school, he is the class clown. He can be lazy, and, although he does have many potential talents, he doesn't use them much. His best friend isLanny Onasis, who never speaks, but Matt can understand him anyway. Matt is sometimes menaced by a bully named Heywood Biggs. Matt plays organized soccer, although he's not very good at it.[6]He can also play the guitar.[7] Matt's Friday night bedtime is nine o'clock.[6] Matt does not like Jo's chicken noodle casserole.[1] Matt does not like carrots in his soup. Whether he doesn't like carrots, or simply doesn't want carrots in his soup, is unclear.[8]

characters.name: Melina Bianco
characters.description: Melina Bianco(Carly Schroeder) isMatt'sfriend who loves gettingLannyand Matt into trouble and eventually becomes Matt's love interest. She was first introduced as Matt's enemy, but they both began to have feelings for each other. Matt and Melina have had multiple playdates scheming up evil genius ideas. She started Kindergarten in 1996 in the same class as Matt.

characters.name: Melina Bianco
characters.description: Melina Bianco(Carly Schroeder) isMatt'sfriend who loves gettingLannyand Matt into trouble and eventually becomes Matt's love interest. She was first introduced as Matt's enemy, but they both began to have feelings for each other. Matt and Melina have had multiple playdates scheming up evil genius ideas. characters.name: Miranda Sanchez
characters.description: Daniela(Mother)Stevie(Sister)Carlos Delgado(Cousin)Grandpa (Grandfather)[1] David Gordon Claire Miller Miranda is smart and witty, and sometimes doesn't get along with Gordo. She and Lizzie were friends with Kate for a while, but Kate gained popularity and became their nemesis. She regularly hosts Lizzie and Gordo at her house on Friday nights, with popcorn and a movie rental.[6]Matt had a crush on her in the episodeWorking Girl. It is stated in many episodes that Miranda and her family are from Mexico and they follow all its cultures and holidays. The Halloween episode shows her family celebrating The Day Of The Dead. She also knows some Spanish when the gang attempt to go on a Spanish game show, but not enough to be very helpful. Her absence in the last few episodes andThe Lizzie McGuire Moviewas due to her vacationing in Mexico. Facts about Miranda: She is an excellent singer.[7]She is a fan of Christina Aguilera[8]and dreams of becoming a backup singer.[4]Her aptitude test suggests a career as a Navy seal.[4]Miranda enjoys shopping at Hot Topic.[2]Miranda is apparently a fan of Tom Cruise[9]and Britney Spears.[10]She likes flan.[7]With one exception, Miranda has never been tongue-tied around boys.[11]She does not like coconut donuts. Whether she doesn't like coconut, or simply doesn't want coconut on her donuts, is unclear.[12] Miranda hates writing on chalkboards, because it makes her hands powdery and dry.[10] She can play several musical instruments, including the autoharp[12]and the violin.

characters.name: Miss Ungermeyer
characters.description: Angela Ungermeyeris the principal atHillridge High School. She appears inThe Lizzie McGuire Movie.

characters.name: Mr. Dig
characters.description: Digby Sellers (aka Mr. Dig)isLizzie'scool, laid-back substitute teacher. In his lessons he implies that he has educated celebrities like Frankie Muniz and Christina Aguilera. He befriendsSam McGuirein one of the episodes and datesMatt'steacher,Miss Chapman. He has his own unconventional way of teaching subjects, and doesn't use a lesson plan. He does not "believe in" tests, quizzes, examinations, or grades.[1] His family originated in Tobago, and although he was born in East Lansing, Michigan, home of the Michigan State Spartans, he grew up as a fan of the Michigan Wolverines.[2]

characters.name: Mr. Escobar
characters.description: Mr. Escobaris a teacher atHillridge Junior High School. He was played by the late Daniel Escobar, who died from diabetes in 2013. Mr. Escobar teaches drama[1]and chorus. He also runs detention.[2]He serves as the director of the school graduation ceremony.[3] He is an amateur playwright,[2]and wrote the school play, "Greasier."[1]

characters.name: Mr. Pettus
characters.description: Mr. Pettusis a science teacher atHillridge Junior High School. When he was a student, his aptitude test suggested a career in animal husbandry entertainment (rodeo clown).[1]

characters.name: Mrs. Miller
characters.description:
Mrs. Milleris the mother ofKate Sanders'sbest friend,Claire Miller.
Just like her daughter, Mrs. Miller is very stylish and fashionable -- but, unlike Claire, Mrs. Miller appears to be a genuinely kind and friendly person. In fact, she seems to be largely unaware of her daughter's cold attitude towards others (such as Lizzie McGuire and her friends).

characters.name: Mrs. Miller
characters.description:
Mrs. Milleris the mother ofKate Sanders'sbest friend,Claire Miller.
Just like her daughter, Mrs. Miller is very stylish and fashionable -- but, unlike Claire, Mrs. Miller appears to be a genuinely kind and friendly person. In fact, she seems to be largely unaware of her daughter's cold attitude towards others (such as Lizzie McGuire and her friends).

characters.name: Mrs. Wortman
characters.description: Mrs. Wortmanis a teacher atHillridge Junior High School. She teaches Algebra, and among her students areLizzie,Gordo,MirandaandAngel Lieberman.[1]

characters.name: Paolo Valisari
characters.description: Paolo Valisariis the main antagonist ofThe Lizzie McGuire Movie. He is a famous and handsome Italian pop star who plans on using Lizzie to avoid being sued for a contract breech. Paolo and his singing partner/girlfriend,Isabella Parigi, were to present an award at the International Music Video Awards, but they had a falling out (possibly because of his lip-syncing). For revenge, Paolo takes matters into his own hands. When Lizzie and her graduating class arrive in Rome for their trip, Lizzie strays away from her class and meets Paolo at a wishing well. Paolo notices Lizzie's resemblance to Isabella. They agree to meet the next day at the Trevi Fountain. Lizzie pretends to be sick and sneaks off to meet Paolo, but they are nearly caught. They elude her class with Paolo's bodyguard, Sergi. Paolo and his singing partner/girlfriend,Isabella Parigi, were to present an award at the International Music Video Awards, but they had a falling out (possibly because of his lip-syncing). For revenge, Paolo takes matters into his own hands. When Lizzie and her graduating class arrive in Rome for their trip, Lizzie strays away from her class and meets Paolo at a wishing well. Paolo notices Lizzie's resemblance to Isabella. They agree to meet the next day at the Trevi Fountain. Lizzie pretends to be sick and sneaks off to meet Paolo, but they are nearly caught. They elude her class with Paolo's bodyguard, Sergi. Paolo tells Lizzie that she has to take Isabella's place at the International Music Video Awards. He says it's to keep Isabella from being sued, and that she has to lip-sync their hit songWhat Dreams Are Made Of. They sneak around preparing for the event while Gordo covers for them, and Lizzie becomes romantically attached to Paolo. Soon the media takes notice of Lizzie, mistaking her for Isabella with dyed hair (Lizzie is blond while Isabella has black hair). Apparently this is part of Paolo's plan. On the day of the concert, Miss Ungermyer discovers one of her students has been skipping, and Gordo takes the fall for Lizzie. Cut out of the class, he heads to the airport, where he meets the real Isabella, who saw a tabloid with Lizzie's picture demanding an explanation. Gordo tells her what happened and learns Paolo's plan to frame Isabella for lip-syncing, humiliating Lizzie in the process. During the concert, Isabella and Gordo race to the IMVA, being held at the Colosseum. They meet up with Lizzie in time to convince her of the truth. As Lizzie and Paolo sing their duet, Gordo and Isabella expose Paolo by unplugging his record tape. Isabella further humiliates Paolo by walking on stage next to Lizzie. Angry at being exposed, Paolo leaves and argues with Sergi, who is disgusted at what he had planned and quits, leaving Paolo to thepaparazzi.
characters.personality_section: Paolo and his singing partner/girlfriend,Isabella Parigi, were to present an award at the International Music Video Awards, but they had a falling out (possibly because of his lip-syncing). For revenge, Paolo takes matters into his own hands. When Lizzie and her graduating class arrive in Rome for their trip, Lizzie strays away from her class and meets Paolo at a wishing well. Paolo notices Lizzie's resemblance to Isabella. They agree to meet the next day at the Trevi Fountain. Lizzie pretends to be sick and sneaks off to meet Paolo, but they are nearly caught. They elude her class with Paolo's bodyguard, Sergi. Paolo tells Lizzie that she has to take Isabella's place at the International Music Video Awards. He says it's to keep Isabella from being sued, and that she has to lip-sync their hit songWhat Dreams Are Made Of. They sneak around preparing for the event while Gordo covers for them, and Lizzie becomes romantically attached to Paolo. Soon the media takes notice of Lizzie, mistaking her for Isabella with dyed hair (Lizzie is blond while Isabella has black hair). Apparently this is part of Paolo's plan. On the day of the concert, Miss Ungermyer discovers one of her students has been skipping, and Gordo takes the fall for Lizzie. Cut out of the class, he heads to the airport, where he meets the real Isabella, who saw a tabloid with Lizzie's picture demanding an explanation. characters.name: Parker McKenzie
characters.description: Well-timed screenshot of Parker as she is complaining about her sister going through all her stuff Parker McKenzieis a girl in Lizzie's class. She was portrayed byChelsea J. Wilson. Lizzie believes that Parker hates her because she sneezed on her macaroni art in second grade[citation needed]and sat on her Titanic lunchbox in fifth grade,[1]but it is more likely that she simply envies Lizzie for her close relationship with Gordo who she has a crush on.[citation needed]Parker is also an extreme environmentalist and a militant vegetarian, once scornfully calling Lizzie a "meat eater."[2] Parker has a skeptical look on her face when Lizzie says, "Maybe I could borrow something from you."
